The movie in of itself looks hilarious and very well written/directed/and acted. Yet as the climate in America continues to get worse why are we still making movies about poverty in a "funny" way? This is a narrative that Black people in America especially get fed and we eat it up knowing it's our own lives/ancestry on the screen we are laughing at. Poverty is not funny, it's a very real reality many people face, it's one that causes many to do things they don't and shouldn't have to do. Selling drugs, selling guns, joining gangs, gun violence, drug addiction, mental health issues. It's all correlated, we gotta stop laughing at this shit on the screen and really understand that this isn't funny anymore....ITS REAL LIFE!!!!!!! Spend your money creating a better reality not a movie that makes fun of the very real life experiences many people of color go through.
